zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Bermingham於1295年出生在英國史洛普爾維林頓時，她的父親William de Bermingham33歲，母親Matilda Deincourt27歲。她和Fulk II Pembridge至少有2個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於1326年3月17日在英國英格蘭享年31歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Bermingham于1295年出生在英国史洛普爾維林頓时，她的父亲William de Bermingham33岁，母亲Matilda Deincourt27岁。她和Fulk II Pembridge至少有2个儿子和5个女儿。她去世于1326年3月17日在英国英格兰享年31岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bermingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Sinead.
Irish (of English origin): variant of   Birmingham  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Ireland.

History: The Bermingham family take their surname from lands held at what is now a great city in the West Midlands of England, but it was brought to Ireland as early as the 12th century. The Norman baron Robert de Bermyngeham was a follower of Strongbow (see Clare ); his name is found on a number of Irish charters between 1175 and 1179. The family was also known as Mac Fheorais (see Corish ) in Ireland, named for Piers (Gaelic Feoras) de Bermyngeham, the father of Robert.

variants.name: Birmingham
meanings: English: habitational name from Birmingham in the West Midlands. In Domesday Book the name is already found as Bermingeham, but it seems likely that it was originally Beornmundingahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the people of (-inga-) Beornmund’, a personal name composed of the elements beorn ‘young man, warrior’ + mund ‘protection’. This surname is established in Ireland (see Bermingham ).

variants.name: Corish
meanings: shortened form of Mac Fheorais ‘son of Piers’. The name was borne by a Connacht family descended from Piers de Bermingham, second lord of Athenry in Galway (died c. 1309). Compare Bermingham .
shortened form of Mac Muirghis ‘son of Maurice’, a personal name introduced to Ireland by the Normans. Compare Morrissey .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Bermingham was born in 1295, in Wellington, Shropshire, England, her father, William de Bermingham, was 33 and her mother, Matilda Deincourt, was 27. She had at least 2 sons and 5 daughters with Fulk II Pembridge. She died on 17 March 1326, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 31.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Bermingham est née en 1295, à Wellington, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Bermingham, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Matilda Deincourt, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles avec Fulk II Pembridge. Elle est décédée le 17 mars 1326, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 31 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Bermingham 1295 in Wellington, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Bermingham 33 und ihre Mutter Matilda Deincourt 27. Sie hatte mit Fulk II Pembridge mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. März 1326 im Alter von 31 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Bermingham è nata il nel 1295 nel Wellington, Shropshire, England, sua padre, William de Bermingham, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Matilda Deincourt, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie con Fulk II Pembridge. Morì il 17 marzo 1326, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 31 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Berminghamが1295年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエリントンで生まれた時，父William de Berminghamは33歳，母Matilda Deincourtは27歳であった。彼女はFulk II Pembridgeとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1326年3月17日にイギリスイギリスで31歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Bermingham(이)가 1295년 영국 슈럽실르 웰잉턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Bermingham(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda Deincourt(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Fulk II Pembridge(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1326년 3월 17일 영국 잉글랜드에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Bermingham nasceu em 1295, em Wellington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Bermingham, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Deincourt, tinha 27 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas com Fulk II Pembridge. Ela faleceu em 17 de março de 1326, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 31 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Bermingham родилась в 1295 г. в местности веллингтон, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Bermingham было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda Deincourt, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей от Fulk II Pembridge. Она умерла 17 марта 1326 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 31 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Bermingham nació en 1295, en Wellington, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Bermingham, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Matilda Deincourt, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas con Fulk II Pembridge. Murió el 17 de marzo de 1326, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 31 años.
